verification_method: arXiv PDF and abstract were read through arxiv.org.
Reference repositories were verified with git ls-remote.
Kernel paths were sampled with shallow sparse clones in a user-selected temporary directory.
GitHub web search was used for public repository discovery when direct API search hit anonymous rate limits.

reference_repositories.name: AI-Infra-Auto-Driven-SKILLS gpu-kernel-ako4all references
reference_repositories.do_not_copy_rule: Use as workflow and debugging guidance. Do not copy target kernel implementation code from any reference without explicit license and attribution handling.

reference_repositories.name: CUTLASS and CuTe DSL
read_when: CUTLASS GEMM, convolution, epilogue, and collective patterns
CuTe C++ tensor algebra and layout composition
SM90/SM100 Tensor Core, TMA, WGMMA, and block-scaled GEMM references
reference_repositories.do_not_copy_rule: Use as a primary design and measurement reference for CUTLASS/CuTe/CuTe DSL work. Do not copy target candidate code without explicit license and attribution handling.

reference_repositories.name: CUDA Samples
read_when: memory hierarchy and cooperative groups examples
CUDA Graphs, streams, events, and library interop
reference_repositories.do_not_copy_rule: Use as official API examples and smoke-test patterns, not as target candidate code.

reference_repositories.name: CUDA Core Compute Libraries
read_when: CUB block, warp, scan, reduce, load/store, and radix patterns
libcu++ synchronization, memory model, and utility primitives
reference_repositories.do_not_copy_rule: Use for primitive selection, correctness oracles, and benchmark patterns. Do not copy library internals into a candidate.

reference_repositories.name: CUDA Library Samples
read_when: official CUDA library benchmark harness structure
reference_repositories.do_not_copy_rule: Use as official library API reference and baseline harness material.

reference_repositories.name: cuDNN Frontend
read_when: cuDNN backend graph and frontend API usage
reference_repositories.do_not_copy_rule: Use as cuDNN API and baseline harness reference, not as target candidate code.

reference_repositories.name: NVBench
read_when: latency, throughput, occupancy, and sweep configuration
benchmark result schema and statistical reporting
reference_repositories.do_not_copy_rule: Use for benchmark methodology and harness design.

reference_repositories.name: CUDA Tile IR
read_when: tensor core targeting and tile scheduling
reference_repositories.do_not_copy_rule: Use as compiler and scheduling prior art, not as target candidate code.

reference_repositories.name: Triton
read_when: Triton language semantics and compiler behavior
reference_repositories.do_not_copy_rule: Use as language and compiler reference for Triton lanes.

reference_repositories.name: TileLang
read_when: GEMM, attention, and elementwise kernel examples
reference_repositories.do_not_copy_rule: Use as DSL and schedule-design reference only.

reference_repositories.name: GPU MODE reference kernels
read_when: short-form CUDA/Triton kernels with clear baselines
reference_repositories.do_not_copy_rule: Use for competition problem design and measurement style. Do not copy submitted solutions into candidates.

reference_repositories.name: GPU MODE KernelBot
reference_repositories.do_not_copy_rule: Use for agent-loop and benchmark workflow ideas, not as candidate implementation source.

reference_repositories.name: GPU MODE Triton Puzzles
read_when: program id, masks, blocks, reductions, and memory behavior
reference_repositories.do_not_copy_rule: Use as educational reference for Triton lanes.

reference_repositories.name: ThunderKittens
read_when: tile primitives and warpgroup-level kernel patterns
reference_repositories.do_not_copy_rule: Use as prior art and design inspiration, not as direct candidate code.

reference_repositories.name: CUDA GEMM Optimization
read_when: shared memory, vectorized load/store, and tiling experiments
reference_repositories.do_not_copy_rule: Use as educational optimization progression and measurement reference.

reference_repositories.name: Colfax CUTLASS kernels
reference_repositories.do_not_copy_rule: Use as tutorial companion reference with attribution.

reference_repositories.name: ModernGPU
read_when: merge, scan, sort, load-balancing, and segmented algorithms
older but still useful CUDA performance patterns
reference_repositories.do_not_copy_rule: Use as algorithmic prior art and primitive design reference.

reference_repositories.name: Hugging Face Kernels
reference_repositories.do_not_copy_rule: Use for packaging and integration structure, not as direct candidate source.

reference_repositories.name: FlashInfer
read_when: Blackwell FMHA or grouped GEMM comparison
MoE, rope, norm, sampling, quantization baselines
reference_repositories.do_not_copy_rule: Treat as inspiration and baseline reference. Do not copy code into a KernelPilot candidate unless license and attribution are explicitly handled.

reference_repositories.name: FlashAttention
reference_repositories.do_not_copy_rule: Use as a reference implementation family and comparison source, not as direct candidate code.

reference_repositories.name: hi-ops / HPC Ops
read_when: Tencent hi-ops attention, group GEMM, normalization, RoPE, activation, or fused MoE references
operator-level API and test design for LLM CUDA kernels
reference_repositories.do_not_copy_rule: Use as a reference source for hypotheses and tests. Do not copy implementation code into a KernelPilot candidate without explicit license and attribution handling.

reference_repositories.name: DeepGEMM
read_when: JIT GEMM and grouped GEMM kernels
DeepSeek-style attention logits and MegaGEMM/MegaMoE layout references
reference_repositories.do_not_copy_rule: Reference only. Treat DeepGEMM kernels as external prior art and use fresh KernelPilot candidates for target tasks.

official_documents.name: CUDA C++ Programming Guide
read_when: validating synchronization, memory, and launch assumptions

official_documents.name: CUDA C++ Best Practices Guide
read_when: turning profiler observations into optimization hypotheses
checking whether a kernel is memory-bound or compute-bound

official_documents.name: PTX ISA
read_when: checking instruction availability and operand constraints

official_documents.name: CUDA Binary Utilities
read_when: debugging compiler output and register usage

official_documents.name: Nsight Compute Profiling Guide
read_when: profiling a candidate before changing tiling

official_documents.name: CUTLASS Documentation
read_when: choosing tile shapes, schedules, and epilogues

official_documents.name: CUDA Architecture Tuning Guides
read_when: adapting a kernel to a specific GPU architecture
checking generation-specific tensor core and memory features

official_documents.name: Blackwell Tuning Guide
read_when: checking architecture-specific occupancy and shared-memory limits

expert_blogs.name: NVIDIA Developer Blog: CUDA C++
read_when: looking for official optimization narratives and examples

expert_blogs.name: NVIDIA Developer Blog: CUTLASS
read_when: looking for official CUTLASS optimization context

expert_blogs.name: Simon Boehm: CUDA Matrix Multiplication
read_when: building a simple-to-advanced GEMM optimization ladder

expert_blogs.name: Lei Mao: CUDA Programming
read_when: checking practical CUDA optimization explanations and small reproducers

expert_blogs.name: Yifan Yang: CUDA Matrix Multiplication
read_when: reasoning about GEMM bottlenecks and register tiling choices

target_project_policy.source_use: The target repository is supplied by the user in each task or prompt. It may be used for integration, tests, benchmarks, and correctness oracles after a candidate exists, but it is not part of the reference repository catalog.
